summary: The docker/bake-action build failed because a wget call inside the Dockerfile received an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway response from GitHub when trying to download the nydus static binary. This caused the Docker build step to exit with code 1, aborting the entire bake.
root_cause: wget returned HTTP 502 Bad Gateway from github.com while downloading the nydus release artifact inside Dockerfile line 336, causing the RUN step to fail with exit code 1.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: The wget command inside the Docker build received a 502 error from GitHub, directly causing the build step to fail.

evidence.quote: ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This is the exact process that failed with exit code 1, confirming the wget failure propagated as a build error.
Identifies the exact Dockerfile line (336) where the failing wget RUN instruction is defined.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: GitHub Actions reported this as the terminal error that caused the bake action to fail.

evidence.quote:
0.091 Connecting to github.com (140.82.113.3:443)
0.149 wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: The connection to GitHub was established but the server returned a 502, indicating a transient upstream/proxy error at GitHub's CDN at the time of the run.

suggested_fix: Re-run the job; the 502 is a transient GitHub CDN/proxy error. If it recurs, consider adding retry logic around the wget command in Dockerfile line 336 (e.g., using a retry loop or switching to curl with --retry flags).

summary: unsupported_context_too_large: context (896814 chars) exceeds shim cap (480000)

A Jest test assertion failed in test/config/get.ts within the pnpm package. The test expected the registry URL 'https://my-org.registry.example.com' but received 'https://my-org.registry.example.com/' (with a trailing slash), causing the test suite to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The test 'pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc' failed because pnpm config get now returns the scoped registry URL with a trailing slash, while the test asserts the value without a trailing slash.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: Exact assertion mismatch — the received value has a trailing slash that the expected value does not, directly identifying the failure.
This is the failing assertion at test/config/get.ts:32, confirming it is a toBe equality check that fails due to the trailing slash difference.
Confirms the test file that failed and that it is a Jest test failure.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly one test failed, consistent with the single assertion failure identified.
Confirms the CI job failed because the jest test command for the pnpm package exited with code 1.

suggested_fix: Update the assertion in test/config/get.ts at line 32 to expect the registry URL with a trailing slash ('https://my-org.registry.example.com/'), or fix the pnpm config get command to strip the trailing slash from scoped registry URLs before outputting them.
